title: Complete your Self Assessment tax return for 5 April 2024 or earlier
body: " You can file your Self Assessment tax return online.   The SA100 is the main tax return for individuals. Use it to file your tax return for:   student loan repayments interest and dividends UK pensions and annuities paying into registered pension schemes charitable giving claiming Blind Person’s Allowances High Income Child Benefit Charge, if you pay it through Self Assessment Marriage Allowance claiming a repayment   If you complete a paper tax return  You’ll need to post your tax return to HMRC.  You may also need to include supplementary pages for certain types of income.  If you live in the UK send to:   Self AssessmentHM Revenue & CustomsBX9 1ASUnited Kingdom   If you live outside the UK send to:   HM Revenue & CustomsBenton Park ViewNewcastle Upon TyneNE98 1ZZUnited Kingdom   HMRC must receive your paper tax return by the deadline.  If you miss the paper filing deadline, send your return online instead to avoid a penalty.  Supplementary pages  SA101: Additional information  SA102: Employment  SA102M: Ministers of religion  SA102MP: Parliament  SA102MLA: Northern Ireland Legislative Assembly  SA102MS: The National Assembly for Wales  SA102MSP: Scottish Parliament  SA103S: Self-employment (short)  SA103F: Self-employment (full)  SA103L: Lloyd’s underwriters  SA104S: Partnership (short)  SA104F: Partnership (full)  SA105: UK property  SA106: Foreign  SA107: Trusts etc  SA108: Capital Gains summary  SA109: Residence, remittance basis etc  SA110: Tax calculation summary  Related forms and guidance  Tax on your private pension contributions  Tell HMRC about a Student Loan in your tax return "
